sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value_list LIST<INTEGER>, note VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value_list, note, created_at) VALUES (1, [1, 2, 3], 'first', '2023-01-01 00:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value_list, note, created_at) VALUES (2, [4, 5, 6], 'second', '2023-01-02 00:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value_list, note, created_at) VALUES (3, [7, 8, 9, NULL], 'third', '2023-01-03 00:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value_list, note, created_at) VALUES (4, [], 'fourth', '2023-01-04 00:00:00');
SELECT id, reduce(value_list, (x, y) -> x + y) AS reduced_value FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING reduced_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT T1.id, (SELECT list_reduce(value_list, (x, y) -> x * y) FROM test_table AS T2 WHERE T2.id = T1.id) AS multiplied_value FROM test_table AS T1;
SELECT id, value_list FROM test_table WHERE (value_list @> [1, 2]) AND (note LIKE 'f%') ORDER BY id;
SELECT id, reduce(value_list, (x, y) -> x + y) AS total_value FROM test_table WHERE created_at > '2023-01-01' GROUP BY id HAVING total_value > 10;
SELECT T1.id, T1.value_list FROM test_table T1 JOIN test_table T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T2.note @> 'second' AND reduce(T2.value_list, (x, y) -> x - y) < 2;
WITH temp AS (SELECT id, reduce(value_list, (x, y) -> x + y) AS sum_value FROM test_table GROUP BY id) SELECT sum_value FROM temp WHERE sum_value IS NOT NULL;
